items.title: From cereal to clothing, see how the price of everyday items has changed

children.content: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

gallery.altText: A group of everyday items; fish, light bulb, coffee etc

thumbnail.altText: A group of everyday items; fish, light bulb, coffee etc

items.title: Israel’s massive strike was opposite of surgical, Lebanon deputy PM says

children.content: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

image.altText: Soldiers standing on top of tanks with a border wall in the distance and a green hill

items.title: 'Someone knows something': Search for missing 5yo Sharon enters fifth day

children.content: Northern Territory Police Commissioner Martin Dole says he is still adamant there are people who have knowledge of the whereabouts of Jefferson Lewis, the man at the centre of a search for missing Alice Springs five-year-old Sharon Granites.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

images.altText: A close-up shot of a woman with short grey gair and black glasses.

articles.title: First report from antisemitism, Bondi royal commission to be released

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will deliver its first report, two months after it began and less than six months after the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Floral tributes at Bondi Beach on day seven after terror attack

children.content: The antisemitism and social cohesion royal commission's interim report has been handed to the governor-general, two months after the inquiry began. Follow live.

items.title: Antisemitism interim report handed to governor-general

updates.title: Antisemitism royal commission interim report handed to governor-general
Leeser urges Australians to make submission to antisemitism royal commission
Wilson says CGT discount, negative gearing changes won't create 'opportunity'

items.title: Coalition helps clear the way for One Nation in Farrer

children.content: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

image.altText: Politician Pauline Hanson gestures to the camera

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Hegseth says Iran war is 'not a quagmire' as cost of conflict revealed

children.content: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth has defended the war against Iran in his first testimony to Congress since it started, instead attacking Democrats as "feckless". Follow live.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth with puckered lips, mid-speech, holding up two fingers on his left hand while sitting in congress.

items.title: Has the world’s most powerful cartel met its end?

children.content: A United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for 66 years.

articles.title: ASX to fall as price of oil surges to more than $US120 a barrel

children.content: The Australian share market is set to open lower in morning trading as oil prices surge. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

articles.title: China to help Australia with jet fuel supplies, Wong says

children.content: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

image.altText: A woman stands in front of the Australian flag.

gallery.altText: A blue barrel bearing the logo of OPEC

thumbnail.altText: A blue barrel bearing the logo of OPEC

items.title: Fuel shock pushes civil contractors to cut jobs and pause works

children.content: Civil construction firms warn surging diesel and material costs driven by the global oil crisis will force lay-offs and project delays, with nearly a third considering winding down.

image.altText: Diesel is pumped into a black ute

items.title: 'It's a safety issue': Construction industry faces gambling crisis

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

image.altText: Men in high-viz and hard hats stand around building supplied chatting.

items.title: I don't want cold pasta for dinner but my appliances aren't cutting it

children.content: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

image.altText: A white woman's face is reflected in a shiny black microwave window

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Renewables and batteries drive down fossil fuel use despite record electricity demand

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: Melbourne rush-hour chaos as hundreds of rail passengers left stranded

children.content: A rail equipment fault caused havoc on the V/Line Geelong Line on Wednesday night, with rail operators warning of more delays today.

items.title: Trump's would-be assassin took selfie 'armed to the teeth', court told

children.content: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

image.altText: Cole Tomas Allen taking a selfie in a mirror with various weapons on his person.

items.title: Insurers among group proposing cheaper premiums for disaster-resistant homes

children.content: As about 1.6 million households experience insurance unaffordability stress, a disparate alliance of stakeholders proposes cheaper premiums for home owners who upgrade their properties to withstand natural disasters.

image.altText: New houses sit on a hillside in a housing estate at Albion Park.

items.title: Driving along this outback road could land you in trouble with bull dust

children.content: Journeying through the Binns Track in remote central Australia, Rae Johnston learns why you should always travel with mates.

items.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?

children.content: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

items.title: Gifted, goofy, gone too soon: Jeff Buckley's genius remembered

children.content: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

image.altText: Young brunette man with stubble poses in a sequined jacket with an old-timey hand microphone and blue backdrop

items.title: King visits 9/11 memorial as he tours New York

children.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London

children.content: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

image.altText: A man in high vis and wearing a kippah talks on the phone

items.title: 'Waiting for me to die': Pension stripped from man with disability

children.content: Ray Noakes was put on the disability pension after he was deemed unable to work. Then he was put on Jobseeker, which he lost because he could not work. Now he wants compensation.

image.altText: A man standing in a doorway with a hallway and back door behind him

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

articles.title: What we know about the fatal plane crash in Adelaide

children.content: Investigations are underway into a light plane crash at an airport in Adelaide's north that claimed two lives and injured 10 others.

image.altText: Smoke among hangars and grass at an airport

items.title: Cases of potentially deadly disease triple in the NT

children.content: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

image.altText: A needle injecting a vaccine into a patient's arm.

items.title: 'Accident' helps identify giant 'shark-toothed' dinosaur's skull fragment

children.content: Two fossils found in Thailand are named as long-missing pieces of a "shark-toothed" dinosaur after being identified partly by accident.

image.altText: an illustration of a dinosaur skull with bone fragments overlaid where the brain would be

items.title: 'No offence to Tassie': The infamous Sirengate mess 20 years on

children.content: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

items.title: Just one rental in Australia affordable for JobSeekers like Jess

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

image.altText: A university student smiling

items.title: 'Free Palestine' party plans to funnel votes to Victorian conservatives

children.content: Victorian voters are being urged to look out for "misleading" party names, as right-wing activists seek to redirect progressive votes to conservative parties.

image.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: Prized 'whale vomit' perfume ingredient washes up on WA beach

children.content: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

gallery.altText: Bald man holding black and brown circular object size of a coconut

thumbnail.altText: Bald man holding black and brown circular object size of a coconut

items.title: Electrifying your home comes with savings, but conversion costs stopping many

children.content: A new report says the money consumers could save by converting from gas to electricity is set to increase over the coming decades, sparking calls for government regulation to encourage the switch.

image.altText: A yellow sign on a wooden post which reads 'we're electrifying!'

items.title: Interpreter shortage leaving non-English speakers at risk in court

children.content: As Brisbane courts face delays and backlogs, cases involving people with limited English are being adjourned when interpreters are unavailable — a problem advocates warn is set to worsen.

image.altText: A graphic showing the word "justice" in English and five other languages.

items.title: Thought to be introduced in WWII, this invasive species is smothering Vanuatu's forests

children.content: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and earning an income for its people at the same time.

gallery.altText: Chief Skip wearing bright orange tshirt cutting down green leaved woody vine.

thumbnail.altText: Chief Skip wearing bright orange tshirt cutting down green leaved woody vine.

items.title: 'Gobsmacking': How a council's push to work four days a week came unstuck

children.content: It wanted to be the first Australian council to usher in a four-day work week. Documents show there was never a standalone business case to back the proposal — but there was a tight grip on messaging.

image.altText: An aerial view of a city during sunset.

items.title: NT government accused of hiding long-term watch house detentions

children.content: Just weeks after the CLP government insisted the Northern Territory's watch house crisis was over, the NT's Aboriginal justice agency has accused it of renaming parts of a Darwin watch house to conceal that people are still being held there long term.

image.altText: Low-angle shot of Darwin city watch house building, with blue and white checkered tiles on front of building in view.

items.title: Officer who laughed at prison death among six sanctioned by WA Police

children.content: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

image.altText: JAIL

items.title: New study suggest Australians largely support renewable transition

children.content: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

image.altText: Wind turbines over the rolling hills.

items.title: APRA Music Awards winners for 2026 announced

children.content: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.
